xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AddressOfRegisteredOfficeOfCom: Plant-7, No. 143/A, Jigani Link Road Bommasandra Industrial area, Anekal Taluk, Bengaluru – 560 105, Karnataka, India
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AddressOfCorporateOfficeOfComp: Plant-7, No. 143/A, Jigani Link Road Bommasandra Industrial area, Anekal Taluk, Bengaluru – 560 105, Karnataka, India
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescriptionOfBusinessActivity: Intricate and critical precision-engineered components used in both automotive and non-automotive sectors.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ProductOrServiceSoldByTheEntit: Manufacture of parts and accessories for motor vehicles
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ABriefOnTypesOfCustomersExplan: Sansera is an engineering-driven, integrated manufacturer of complex and critical precision-engineered components serving both automotive and non-automotive sectors. The Company manufactures a broad spectrum of these components for diverse applications across these industries. Sansera’s global customer base includes Bajaj, Maruti Suzuki, Toyota, Royal Enfield, KTM, Daimler Group, VW Group, Volvo, BMW, Ferrari, Ducati, and General Motors , among others. The Company constantly works to expand its product portfolio in the following areas: a) Technology-agnostic applications within the automotive sector such as suspension, chassis, driveline, and braking systems, among others, catering to ICE and EV powertrain platforms. b) Non-automotive sectors, including aerospace, defence, agriculture, industrial automation, and stationary engines, among others.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheHoldingOrSubsidiaryAs: Fitwel Tools and Forgings Private Limited
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RemarksStakeHolderGroupFromWho: Grievances about shop floor conditions, welfare, safety
Grievances about shop floor conditions, welfare, safety
Grievances about shop floor conditions, welfare, safety
Grievances about shop floor conditions, welfare, safety
General notices, show cause notices, fines, interest, and damages
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Automotive parts manufacturing is a high-emission activity subject to scrutiny from key stakeholders including customers, investors, and governments. Customers may require commitments to lower greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions or set emission-related conditions for business dealings. Some investors focused on environmental, social, and governance (ESG) criteria may invest only in companies that achieve GHG reduction targets. Additionally, the government is working on implementing a cap-and-trade emissions trading scheme in India.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: GHG emissions can be mitigated by: (1) conducting energy audits to identify areas for energy reduction and (2) increasing the sourcing of renewable energy. In the current financial year, a total of 60% of energy was sourced from renewable sources.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Improper handling of hazardous waste by waste management contractors presents risks to land, water bodies, and biodiversity. Adopting circular economy principles for waste recycling could provide Sansera with a valuable opportunity to minimise waste generation.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Ensuring that hazardous waste is handled and disposed of only by contractors authorised by the State Pollution Control Board. Circular economy initiatives include (1) recycling of discarded scrap steel and (2) achieving zero waste to landfill.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Sansera’s manufacturing process consumes a substantial amount of energy, and conserving energy not only improves our financial performance but also supports achieving our emissions reduction goals.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Identifying areas for energy reduction and implementing appropriate measures — all Sansera plants are certified under ISO 50001 (2018) for Energy Management Systems.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: The risk involves enhanced solutions that address new requirements, especially environmental and social aspects, technological advancements, upgrades, or current market demands.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Greater emphasis on the manufacturing of EV products.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Occupational Health & Safety (OH&S) impacts both individuals and the local community and has the potential to disrupt operations. Risks to employees and workers include physical hazards, chemical exposure, vehicle movement, ergonomic strain, respiratory risks, and noise exposure. Fire hazards pose significant risks to employees, workers, and the neighbouring community.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Sansera has implemented multiple measures to mitigate occupational health and safety risks, including: 1. Adoption of an organisation-wide Integrated Policy on Environment, Health, Safety, and Quality Management System. 2. Conducting trainings and internal audits for all SOPs related to Operational Health and Safety. 3. Maintenance of HIRA (Hazard Identification and Risk Assessment). 4. Certification of all plants to ISO 45001. 5. Periodic medical check-ups for all employees and workers. 6. Monitoring of environmental parameters in accordance with PCB standards.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI) offer Sansera important opportunities to create a more inclusive and equitable workplace, enhance employee engagement, boost customer satisfaction, and develop a positive brand reputation
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Sansera is actively working to increase the number of female employees across various functions
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Failure to maintain sustainable practices across the supply chain, including responsible sourcing of raw materials, waste management, and adherence to environmental regulations can lead to reputational harm and possible legal repercussions. Ethical and social responsibility risks encompass concerns such as violations of labour rights, human rights abuses, child labour, and unsafe working conditions within supplier facilities.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Sansera has implemented several measures to mitigate supply chain risks, including: 1. Adoption of responsible sourcing policies such as the Supplier Responsible Sourcing of Natural Raw Materials Policy, Sustainable Procurement Policy, Sansera Principles to Enhance Sustainability Performance in the Supply Chain, and the Sansera Policy on Conflict Minerals. 2. Requiring suppliers to commit to these policies, with the exception of the Sansera Policy on Conflict Minerals. 3. Conducting system and sustainability assessments prior to onboarding suppliers.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Risks emerge from potential cyberattacks such as ransomware, malware, and phishing attempts. These attacks could exploit vulnerabilities in networks or software to gain unauthorised access to the Company’s sensitive data or disrupt critical systems. Data breaches may result from external attacks, insider threats, or insufficient security measures, causing unauthorised access, theft, or exposure of confidential information.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Sansera has deployed and implemented various technical tools and controls to mitigate Information Security risks, including but not limited to: 1. End Point and Server Protection: CrowdStrike Falcon Complete (EDR) – provides protection against ransomware, and malware, among others. 2. Network Perimeter: UTP (Unified Threat Protection) safeguards against external threats and intruders. 3. Device Control – manages access to Bluetooth, external USB drives, card readers, and other peripheral devices. 4. Data Loss Prevention (DLP) – monitors and protects sensitive data from unauthorized sharing or leakage. 5. Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) – enhances access security by requiring multiple verification methods. 6. Regular vulnerability assessments and penetration testing to identify and remediate potential security weaknesses. Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) systems for real-time monitoring and incident response. 8. These measures collectively strengthen Sansera’s information security posture and reduce the risk of cyber threats.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Business ethics cover a range of aspects, including corporate governance, employee behaviour, labour relations, customer relationships, and supply chain practices. Failure to maintain ethical standards in these areas can expose the organisation to multiple risks, such as legal, reputational, financial, and market-related risks. With the increasing emphasis on Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) principles, governance-related issues are attracting greater attention from potential investors and key customers.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: To mitigate business ethics risks, various measures have been implemented, including the establishment of a Code of Conduct for the Board of Directors, Independent Directors, Senior Management, and Employees. Additionally, policies on anti-corruption, anti-bribery, and anti-money laundering are in place. A whistle-blower policy has also been implemented to encourage reporting of unethical behavior. These policies are supported by awareness training and grievance redressal systems.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Risks related to transparency and disclosure arise from delays in communicating regulatory and stakeholder-focussed disclosures. Failure to disclose Sansera's financial and ESG information transparently could result in reputational damage and negative stakeholder perceptions. Opportunities exist in providing accurate, transparent, and timely disclosures. Increasing climate-related disclosures offers Sansera a chance to build trust and enhance its reputation among all stakeholders.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: To address risks related to transparency and disclosure, Sansera has published the mandatory BRSR disclosures and is actively promoting ESG disclosures via its website and other channels with its stakeholders.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkOfThePoliciesExplanator: Sansera’ss policies on risk management, code of conduct, CSR, vigil mechanism and whistleblower, nomination and remuneration, POSH, EOHS, quality, energy and sustainable procurement are available at: https://sansera.in/investor-policies, https://sansera.in/general-policies, and https:// sansera.in/nomination-remuneration#

Sansera’ss policies on risk management, code of conduct, CSR, vigil mechanism and whistleblower, nomination and remuneration, POSH, EOHS, quality, energy and sustainable procurement are available at: https://sansera.in/investor-policies, https://sansera.in/general-policies, and https:// sansera.in/nomination-remuneration#

SEL’s policies on risk management, code of conduct, CSR, vigil mechanism and whistle-blower, nomination and remuneration, POSH, EOHS, quality, energy and sustainable procurement can be found at: https://sansera.in/investor-policies , https://sansera.in/general-policies, and https://sansera.in/nomination-remuneration#

SEL’s policies on risk management, code of conduct, CSR, vigil mechanism and whistle-blower, nomination and remuneration, POSH, EOHS, quality, energy and sustainable procurement can be found at: https://sansera.in/investor-policies , https://sansera.in/general-policies, and https://sansera.in/nomination-remuneration#

SEL’s policies on risk management, code of conduct, CSR, vigil mechanism and whistle-blower, nomination and remuneration, POSH, EOHS, quality, energy and sustainable procurement can be found at: https://sansera.in/investor-policies , https://sansera.in/general-policies, and https://sansera.in/nomination-remuneration#

SEL’s policies on risk management, code of conduct, CSR, vigil mechanism and whistle-blower, nomination and remuneration, POSH, EOHS, quality, energy and sustainable procurement can be found at: https://sansera.in/investor-policies , https://sansera.in/general-policies, and https://sansera.in/nomination-remuneration#
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheNationalAndInternatio: IATF 16949: 2016, OECD Guidelines for Multinational Enterprises, UNGC Guiding Principles on Business and Human Rights.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:SpecificCommitmentsGoalsAndTar: Sansera’s goals include significant reductions in emissions and waste, greater adoption of renewable energy, improvements in product responsibility, assurance of occupational safety, advancement of diversity, equity, and inclusion, development of a sustainable supply chain, attainment of ISO 27001 certification at all plants, delivery of comprehensive ethics and compliance training, and deployment of an ESG data management system.

Sansera’s goals include significant reductions in emissions and waste, greater adoption of renewable energy, improvements in product responsibility, assurance of occupational safety, advancement of diversity, equity, and inclusion, development of a sustainable supply chain, attainment of ISO 27001 certification at all plants, delivery of comprehensive ethics and compliance training, and deployment of an ESG data management system.

Sansera’s goals include significant reductions in emissions and waste, greater adoption of renewable energy, improvements in product responsibility, assurance of occupational safety, advancement of diversity, equity, and inclusion, development of a sustainable supply chain, attainment of ISO 27001 certification at all plants, delivery of comprehensive ethics and compliance training, and deployment of an ESG data management system.

Sansera’s goals include significant reductions in emissions and waste, greater adoption of renewable energy, improvements in product responsibility, assurance of occupational safety, advancement of diversity, equity, and inclusion, development of a sustainable supply chain, attainment of ISO 27001 certification at all plants, delivery of comprehensive ethics and compliance training, and deployment of an ESG data management system.

Sansera’s goals include significant reductions in emissions and waste, greater adoption of renewable energy, improvements in product responsibility, assurance of occupational safety, advancement of diversity, equity, and inclusion, development of a sustainable supply chain, attainment of ISO 27001 certification at all plants, delivery of comprehensive ethics and compliance training, and deployment of an ESG data management system.

Sansera’s goals include significant reductions in emissions and waste, greater adoption of renewable energy, improvements in product responsibility, assurance of occupational safety, advancement of diversity, equity, and inclusion, development of a sustainable supply chain, attainment of ISO 27001 certification at all plants, delivery of comprehensive ethics and compliance training, and deployment of an ESG data management system.

Sansera’s goals include significant reductions in emissions and waste, greater adoption of renewable energy, improvements in product responsibility, assurance of occupational safety, advancement of diversity, equity, and inclusion, development of a sustainable supply chain, attainment of ISO 27001 certification at all plants, delivery of comprehensive ethics and compliance training, and deployment of an ESG data management system.

Sansera’s goals include significant reductions in emissions and waste, greater adoption of renewable energy, improvements in product responsibility, assurance of occupational safety, advancement of diversity, equity, and inclusion, development of a sustainable supply chain, attainment of ISO 27001 certification at all plants, delivery of comprehensive ethics and compliance training, and deployment of an ESG data management system.

Sansera’s goals include significant reductions in emissions and waste, greater adoption of renewable energy, improvements in product responsibility, assurance of occupational safety, advancement of diversity, equity, and inclusion, development of a sustainable supply chain, attainment of ISO 27001 certification at all plants, delivery of comprehensive ethics and compliance training, and deployment of an ESG data management system.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PerformanceOfTheEntityAgainstT: Sansera is actively advancing its sustainability objectives through a focussed and structured approach to sustainability across various fronts. Significant achievements include achieving 100% training on the code of conduct and policies under Ethics & Compliance. In Waste Management, Sansera has successfully eliminated 95% of waste to landfill, as the waste is being used as alternate fuel raw material for other industries such as cement, among others. Efforts in Occupational Safety and Health are focussed on implementing safety protocols to achieve a reduction in recordable injuries by 2030. Diversity, Equity & Inclusion initiatives are underway, with current progress showing more than 5.4% women in the workforce, towards a target of 10% by 2030. The Sustainable Supply Chain initiative has been completed for 65% of suppliers, with plans to cover 75% by 2027. Transparency & Disclosure efforts include ongoing implementation of an ESG data management system to enhance reporting and transparency across operations. Sansera has sourced 60% of its electricity from renewable sources such as Solar, Wind and GC Hybrid. Considering the electricity and fuel consumption such as diesel and natural gas, Sansera’s Clean energy accounts to 48% and Intends to achieve 80% by 2030. These efforts underscore Sansera’s commitment to sustainability and responsible business practices, aligning its operations with global ESG standards while continuously striving to meet and exceed its set targets.

xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:StatementByDirectorResponsible: Sansera is committed to being a responsible corporate entity, aiming to make a positive impact on both society and the environment through its core business activities. The company has implemented a robust ESG policy that ensures compliance with local, Indian, and global regulations across all its operations. Dedicated ESG committees have been formed at both the Board and operational levels to design detailed roadmaps with specific targets for waste reduction, adoption of eco-friendly practices, emission reduction, and CSR initiatives. These efforts highlight Sansera's persistent commitment to sustainable growth, excellence in governance, and social responsibility, with the goal of surpassing industry standards and making meaningful contributions to the communities it operates in
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfTheHighestAuthorityRe: Name: F.R Singhvi Address: Sansera Engineering Ltd, 143/A, Jigani Link Road, Bengaluru - 560 105 Email: fr@sansera.in DIN: 00233146
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfSpecifiedCommitteeOfT: Yes. The ESG Committee of the Board is responsible for making decisions on sustainability-related matters. Lakshminarayan M Non-Executive Independent Director DIN: 00064750 Revathy Ashok Non-Executive Independent Director DIN: 00057539 F. R. Singhvi Executive Director DIN: 00233146
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescriptionOfOtherCommitteeFor: SEL complies with all statutory requirements that are relevant to the principles and in the event of any non-compliances, the relevant committee investigates and rectifies the issues. The company has implemented a statutory compliance monitoring tool, ‘Ricago’ for effective monitoring of statutory compliance. Regular audits and reviews are conducted by the company to ensure continuous improvement and adherence to regulatory standards.

SEL complies with all statutory requirements that are relevant to the principles and in the event of any non-compliances, the relevant committee investigates and rectifies the issues. The company has implemented a statutory compliance monitoring tool, ‘Ricago’ for effective monitoring of statutory compliance. Regular audits and reviews are conducted by the company to ensure continuous improvement and adherence to regulatory standards.

SEL complies with all statutory requirements that are relevant to the principles and in the event of any non-compliances, the relevant committee investigates and rectifies the issues. The company has implemented a statutory compliance monitoring tool, ‘Ricago’ for effective monitoring of statutory compliance. Regular audits and reviews are conducted by the company to ensure continuous improvement and adherence to regulatory standards.

The company has implemented a statutory compliance monitoring tool, ‘Ricago’ for effective monitoring of statutory compliance. Regular audits and reviews are conducted by the company to ensure continuous improvement and adherence to regulatory standards.

Regular audits and reviews are conducted by the company to ensure continuous improvement and adherence to regulatory standards.

The company has implemented a statutory compliance monitoring tool, ‘Ricago’ for effective monitoring of statutory compliance. Regular audits and reviews are conducted by the company to ensure continuous improvement and adherence to regulatory standards.

SEL complies with all statutory requirements that are relevant to the principles and in the event of any non-compliances, the relevant committee investigates and rectifies the issues. The company has implemented a statutory compliance monitoring tool, ‘Ricago’ for effective monitoring of statutory compliance. Regular audits and reviews are conducted by the company to ensure continuous improvement and adherence to regulatory standards.

SEL complies with all statutory requirements that are relevant to the principles and in the event of any non-compliances, the relevant committee investigates and rectifies the issues. The company has implemented a statutory compliance monitoring tool, ‘Ricago’ for effective monitoring of statutory compliance. Regular audits and reviews are conducted by the company to ensure continuous improvement and adherence to regulatory standards.

SEL complies with all statutory requirements that are relevant to the principles and in the event of any non-compliances, the relevant committee investigates and rectifies the issues. The company has implemented a statutory compliance monitoring tool, ‘Ricago’ for effective monitoring of statutory compliance. Regular audits and reviews are conducted by the company to ensure continuous improvement and adherence to regulatory standards.

SEL complies with all statutory requirements that are relevant to the principles and in the event of any non-compliances, the relevant committee investigates and rectifies the issues. The company has implemented a statutory compliance monitoring tool, ‘Ricago’ for effective monitoring of statutory compliance. Regular audits and reviews are conducted by the company to ensure continuous improvement and adherence to regulatory standards.

SEL complies with all statutory requirements that are relevant to the principles and in the event of any non-compliances, the relevant committee investigates and rectifies the issues. The company has implemented a statutory compliance monitoring tool, ‘Ricago’ for effective monitoring of statutory compliance. Regular audits and reviews are conducted by the company to ensure continuous improvement and adherence to regulatory standards.

SEL complies with all statutory requirements that are relevant to the principles and in the event of any non-compliances, the relevant committee investigates and rectifies the issues. The company has implemented a statutory compliance monitoring tool, ‘Ricago’ for effective monitoring of statutory compliance. Regular audits and reviews are conducted by the company to ensure continuous improvement and adherence to regulatory standards.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:TopicsOrPrinciplesCoveredUnder: Enterprises Risk Management Programme, ESG Awareness & GHG reduction drive, Succession Planning, Business continuity
Enterprises Risk Management Program, ESG Awareness & GHG reduction drive at Sansera, Succession Planning, Business continuity

Induction & on the job Training, Multi Skill Training, Technical Programs, Management Systems, Customer Specific Requirement Training (CSR) Upskilling, EHS Programs, Health Talks, ESG Awareness & GHG reduction drive at Sansera, HR Policies, Supervisory Development Program, Buddy Program, Coaching, Mentoring & Outbound Program

Induction & on the job Training, Multi Skill Training, Refresher Training, Technical Programs, Management Systems, Customer Specific Requirement Training (CSR) Upskilling, EHS Programs, Health Talks, ESG Awareness & GHG reduction drive at Sansera, HR Policies, Supervisory Development Program, Buddy Program, Coaching, Mentoring & Outbound Program,
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AntiCorruptionOrAntiBriberyPol: Yes, The Company has a zero-tolerance approach towards bribery and corruption. We are committed to fair competition, a free market, and ethical business practices, and reject any contracts involving illegal activities. The Company maintains a high standard of honesty and responsibility in all commercial activities through its ‘Anti-Bribery Policy’. Training on this policy is provided through various methods, including web-based tools, electronic platforms, group sessions, or one-on-one interactions, as needed. Additionally, the Company has established a Vigil Mechanism and Whistle-Blower Policy to facilitate the reporting and investigation of issues related to anti-corruption and anti-bribery. The Code of Conduct for Directors and Senior Management is also available to guide the leadership team on integrity in their business dealings. Sansera Anti-Bribery Policy Code of Conduct for Directors and Senior Management Vigil Mechanism and Whistle-Blower Policy
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkAtAntiCorruptionOrAntiB: Sansera Anti-Bribery Policy Code of Conduct for Directors and Senior Management Vigil Mechanism and Whistle-Blower Policy
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfAnyCorrectiveActionTa: Since there have been no cases of corruption or conflict of interest requiring action by regulators, law enforcement agencies, or judicial institutions, there are no specific corrective actions to detail.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfTheEntityHaveProcesse: Yes, the Directors, Key Managerial Personnel, and Senior Management are expected to exercise professional judgement in their respective roles. They are obligated to disclose any relationships or associations that could potentially lead to a conflict of interest. Additionally, they must avoid involvement in business activities where their relatives may have significant influence. It is their responsibility to safeguard the Company’s assets and ensure these are used solely for authorised business purposes. They are also required to maintain the confidentiality of unpublished price-sensitive information and must not seek personal benefits or assist others to do so. These guidelines are outlined in the Code of Conduct to prevent conflict of interest at the Board level. Conflict of interest is also addressed in the Business Ethics Policy. Sansera Business Ethics, Working Condition and Human Rights Policy Guideline to Enhance Sustainability Code of Conduct for Directors and Senior Management
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple1ExplanatoryText:
Number of days of accounts payables- FY 2024-25- 56.33, FY 2023-24- 54.40
Investments in related parties as % of Total Investments made- FY 2024-25- 100%
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:TopicsOrPrinciplesCoveredUnder: EHS Awareness, Industrial Behaviour, ESG Awareness, Driving Etiquettes, Good Housekeeping Practices, Fire Safety Training, First Aid Training, Hazardous Waste Handling, Technical Trainings, and Training on Customer-Specific Requirements
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfImprovementsInEnviron: Sansera is committed to green energy utilisation and clean development mechanisms. In its new plant, solar panels have been installed, and green building certification has been achieved. During the fiscal year, the Company purchased 7.6 crore units of green energy and invested in energy conservation projects
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheProcessesInPlaceToS: 1. Reuse - Packing bins/ trays/ covers return from customers and suppliers for reuse. 2.Disposal & Recycle - Discorded plastic bins / cans, packing covers and used barrels send to the EPR authorized vendors for recycle
Disposal & Recycle - send to the authorized vendors for recycle
Disposed to authorised vendor for incineration. Used oils are sent to authorised vendor for recycle
Disposal & Recycle - send to the authorized vendors for recycle E.g.: paper, corrugated box, boring scrap, garden waste, Food waste (Sent to Piggeries/ animal farms)
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:IndicateInPutMaterial: There was no recycled/reused material used for production
There was no recycled/reused material used for production
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkOfEqualOppertunityPolic: SEL provides equal employment opportunity without any discrimination on the grounds of age, colour, disability, marital status, nationality, race, religion, sex, or sexual orientation. The Company has a Non-Discrimination Policy, which covers these aspects.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfMechanismAvailableToR:
Yes, the Company has a grievance redressal policy to receive and resolve grievances.
If an employee/worker faces any difficulty that interferes with his/her performance, the concerned personnel can report the same and seek redressal. 1. The employee/worker is expected to follow the appropriate process of informing/discussing with his/her immediate Supervisor as the first step. 2. The aggrieved employee can submit the grievance in writing to the Department Head and HR department, in the event Supervisor fails to resolve the grievance within a week. 3. The employee is allowed to approach the Management through the HR, only if it is not resolved by the Department Head and HR department. 4. Grievances received through the Union committee are discussed periodically. 5. Grievances related to women employees are discussed separately, recorded, and resolved appropriately.

Yes, the Company has a grievance redressal policy to receive and resolve grievances.
If an employee/worker faces any difficulty that interferes with his/her performance, the concerned personnel can report the same and seek redressal. 1. The employee/worker is expected to follow the appropriate process of informing/discussing with his/her immediate Supervisor as the first step. 2. The aggrieved employee can submit the grievance in writing to the Department Head and HR department, in the event Supervisor fails to resolve the grievance within a week. 3. The employee is allowed to approach the Management through the HR, only if it is not resolved by the Department Head and HR department. 4. Grievances received through the Union committee are discussed periodically. 5. Grievances related to women employees are discussed separately, recorded, and resolved appropriately.

Yes, the Company has a grievance redressal policy to receive and resolve grievances.
If an employee/worker faces any difficulty that interferes with his/her performance, the concerned personnel can report the same and seek redressal. 1. The employee/worker is expected to follow the appropriate process of informing/discussing with his/her immediate Supervisor as the first step. 2. The aggrieved employee can submit the grievance in writing to the Department Head and HR department, in the event Supervisor fails to resolve the grievance within a week. 3. The employee is allowed to approach the Management through the HR, only if it is not resolved by the Department Head and HR department. 4. Grievances received through the Union committee are discussed periodically. 5. Grievances related to women employees are discussed separately, recorded, and resolved appropriately.

Yes, the Company has a grievance redressal policy to receive and resolve grievances.
If an employee/worker faces any difficulty that interferes with his/her performance, the concerned personnel can report the same and seek redressal. 1. The employee/worker is expected to follow the appropriate process of informing/discussing with his/her immediate Supervisor as the first step. 2. The aggrieved employee can submit the grievance in writing to the Department Head and HR department, in the event Supervisor fails to resolve the grievance within a week. 3. The employee is allowed to approach the Management through the HR, only if it is not resolved by the Department Head and HR department. 4. Grievances received through the Union committee are discussed periodically. 5. Grievances related to women employees are discussed separately, recorded, and resolved appropriately.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOccupationalHealthAnd: Yes, the Health and Safety Management system covers activities across most of our manufacturing locations, offices, research laboratories and supply chain partner and ensures the protection of environment and health & safety of the Company’s employees, contractors, visitors, and relevant stakeholders. 14 out of 18 plants are covered by ISO - 45001 (TUV NORD).
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DesclosureOfTheProcessesUsedTo: The Company has implemented a Hazard Identification and Risk Assessment (HIRA) system for identifying workplace hazards, undertaking risk assessments, implementing necessary controls to mitigate and address risks. SEL identifies possible workplace incidents using a hazard identification process, followed by a likelihood assessment to estimate their frequency or probability. Measures are then implemented to either prevent these incidents by reducing their likelihood or control them to limit their extent and duration, thereby mitigating adverse effects or consequences. In addition to this, the Company has prepared an Emergency Preparedness Plan, and OCP - Operation control procedure/Standards to minimise work-related hazards, among others. SEL ensures HIRA for all activities and products and conducts HIRA studies on an annual basis or as and when required. The necessary guidelines are implemented in line with ISO 45001.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMeasuresTakenByTheE: The Company is certified as a Great Place to Work for three consecutive years. In addition to this leading certification, the Company has taken multiple measures to ensure a safe and healthy workplace, and a few of them are mentioned below: 1. Provision of appropriate safety gear for workers and monitoring of health through periodic checkups. 2. Strict adherence to safety protocols while on the shop floor. 3. Safety reviews and implementation of learning mechanisms to build a culture of peer sharing of best practices especially in areas of fire safety, NC machines handling, chemical safety, etc. 4. Mock drills are conducted on a scenario basis every month; safety committee meetings are held quarterly. The EHS team conducts toolbox talks with vendors, and there are daily Gemba walks. Additionally, lockout/tagout (LOTO) procedures are implemented, training programs are organized, and health and safety audits are performed monthly. 5. Plant 11 and Plant 9 are IGBC Certified (Platinum)
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfAnyCorrectiveActionTa: SEL follows a structured and systematic approach to safety management, which includes key steps such as incident management, investigation and root cause analysis, risk assessment and mitigation, training and awareness, improved safety policies, monitoring and review, communication and reporting, and compliance with regulations. Under these aspects, we have initiated several measures, including the installation of electrical insulating rubber mats, fire hydrant systems, fire extinguishers, public address systems, fire alarm panels, and directional signages. Additionally, we have implemented CCTV, electric leakage circuit breakers, safety toolbox talks and awareness programs, Health & Safety Skill Olympiad, trainings and mock drills, health and safety advisory, and safety guards and stop sensors for rotary parts in machines.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RemarksforComplaintsExplanator: The Company is certified as a Great Place to Work for three consecutive years. In addition to this leading certification, the Company has taken multiple measures to ensure a safe and healthy workplace, and a few of them are mentioned below: 1. Provision of appropriate safety gear for workers and monitoring of health through periodic checkups. 2. Strict adherence to safety protocols while on the shop floor. 3. Safety reviews and implementation of learning mechanisms to build a culture of peer sharing of best practices especially in areas of fire safety, NC machines handling, chemical safety, etc. 4. Mock drills are conducted on a scenario basis every month; safety committee meetings are held quarterly. The EHS team conducts toolbox talks with vendors, and there are daily Gemba walks. Additionally, lockout/tagout (LOTO) procedures are implemented, training programs are organized, and health and safety audits are performed monthly. 5. Plant 11 and Plant 9 are IGBC Certified (Platinum)

The Company has been certified as a ‘Great Place to Work’ for three consecutive years. Along with this prestigious certification, the Company has implemented several measures to ensure a safe and healthy workplace, including: 1. Provision of appropriate safety gear for workers and health monitoring through periodic checkups. 2. Strict adherence to safety protocols on the shop floor. 3. Safety reviews and the implementation of learning mechanisms to foster a culture of peer sharing of best practices, especially in areas such as fire safety, NC machine handling, and chemical safety. 4. Monthly scenario-based mock drills; quarterly safety committee meetings; toolbox talks by the EHS team with vendors; daily Gemba walks; implementation of lockout/tagout (LOTO) procedures; training programs; and monthly health and safety audits. 5. Plant 11 and Plant 9 are IGBC Certified (Platinum).
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfMeasuresUndertakenByT: The Company ensures remittance of all statutory dues on time. SEL also verifies periodically monthly for contract employees.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfAnyCorrectiveActionTa: After the sustainability assessment of value chain suppliers, a timeline and target are issued to enable corrective actions and close identified gaps. A follow-up audit is conducted to ensure compliance with Sansera’s requirements. Previously, GHG emissions data was not captured; however, it is now actively monitored and submitted to customers as needed. Initially, carbon reduction goals were not included in the EHS objectives, but these goals are now clearly defined. Treated effluent was not considered in environmental aspects and hazard identification but has since been incorporated into the assessment of aspects, impacts, hazards, risks, control measures, opportunities, and impact magnitude. A grievance mechanism was previously unaddressed but is now in place and managed by human resources and the management team. Based on recommendations, smoke detectors have been installed in the goods storage area. Additionally, first aid cases are now analyzed to better understand their root causes.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheProcessesForIdentif: Key stakeholders are determined depending on how the given stakeholder groups have an immediate impact on the operations and workings of the Company or how much of a material influence SEL’s business decisions and results have on them. The Company has identified employees, customers, investors, vendors, contractors, collaborators/technical partners, the local community, and government/regulators as its key stakeholders.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ProvideTheProcessesForConsulta: A board-level ESG committee has been formed by SEL. The ESG committee oversees updating the Board of changes as they occur and soliciting feedback from the Directors. Functional heads and teams interact regularly with key stakeholders such as customers and value chain partners, and in turn keep the ESG committee informed of key developments.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfInstancesAsToHowTheIn: Yes, Sansera regularly engages and collaborates with its key stakeholders, enabling progress on ESG topics and the sharing of updates. The Company also conducts periodic evaluations to revise policies in accordance with regulations and stakeholder feedback. Mechanisms for capturing feedback such as safety committee meetings, grievance committee meetings, canteen committee meetings, and POSH committee meetings, are in place to facilitate discussions on key ESG issues.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfInstancesOfEngagement: Sansera carries out its Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) initiatives through the Sansera Foundation, which leads a range of projects focused on education, healthcare, environmental sustainability, and community development to create long-term positive impacts. https://sansera.in/sansera-foundation
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: Employee Communication through - goal setting and performance appraisal meetings, email, SMS, WhatsApp groups, websites, publications, newsletters, display notices of policies at various locations, covered in training plan, Sansera intranet (IMS,ESSemployee self-service software) whereby employees can access policies, social media such as LinkedIn, Townhall meetings (monthly plant wise), Sunrise meeting, DWM - Daily work Management, Safety committee meetings, POSH meetings, Grievance Committee meetings, canteen committee meetings.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: Long-term strategic plans, training and awareness programmes, health, safety, and engagement initiatives. Promote employee volunteerism and increase safety awareness. Provide awareness on regulatory updates
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: Seminars, conferences, official communication channels, regulatory audits/inspections, environmental compliance, policy intervention, websites, government portals, sheel legal software for communication, Ricago softwarelegal and regulatory updates, compliance escalation software periodically updated, Updates from forums such as Chamber of Commerce - highlights on legal requirements, HR community groups.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: Investors prefer to invest in organisations that demonstrate social and environmental responsible.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: Website, publications, mass media & digital communications, plant visits, conferences, customer surveys, social media, and events
Plant visits, Supplier audits, professional networks, Workshops & trainings, Supplier performance monitoring (monthly),
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: Quality, timely delivery and payments, ISO and OSHA standards followed by the supply chain.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: Seminars/conferences, official communication channels, regulatory audits/inspections, website, government portals and notices.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: Guiding in terms of understanding government schemes in the same area (if any) for increased effectiveness. Resolution of compliance-related queries, if any
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: Meets (of community/local authority and town council/ committee/location head/SWOT council), community visits and projects, partnership with local charities, volunteerism, seminars/ conferences.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: Please refer to the following link for information about the Company's community engagements: https://sansera.in/sanserafoundation
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: Annual reports, press releases, earnings call, investor meets, oneon- one calls and meetings, investor conferences.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheInternalMechanismsI: If an employee faces any difficulty that interferes with his/her performance, the concerned employee can report the same and seek redressal. The aggrieved employee can submit the grievance in writing to the Department Head and HR department. In the event of his/ her supervisor failing to resolve the grievance within a week from the date of representation or if he/she is not satisfied with the decision of the supervisor. The employee is allowed to approach the Management through the HR department only if the grievance is not resolved by the Department Head and HR department. The Management reviews the grievances, and the decisions are conveyed to the aggrieved employee within five working days. Grievances received through the Union Committee are discussed periodically based on requirements in the presence of the Management team and Union team.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MechanismsToPreventAdverseCons: The Company has a policy against any form of discrimination based on race, sex, nationality, religion, age, disability, sexual orientation, medical condition or language, as protected by the law. Additionally, the Company has a Prevention of Sexual Harassment Policy (POSH) and Internal Committees (IC) at each location to address any issues related to sexual harassment at the workplace. The investigation process ensures strict confidentiality of the matter, the complainant and witnesses. In case the complainant feels targeted, he/she can inform the Internal Committees for required action to be taken to ensure the complainant’s safety.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfAnyCorrectiveActionsT: No concerns were raised during the assessments that required corrective action.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfABusinessProcessBeing: The Company strives to sensitise employees and workers on human rights through its training programs. As there were no grievances related to human rights, no business process was modified or introduced.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfTheScopeAndCoverageOf: Sustainability audit conducted by the customers. The Responsible Business Alliance conducted by OEMs covered a range of topics including human rights, child labour, forced labour, working hours, overtime control, health and safety, freedom of movement, and wages.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfAnyCorrectiveActionsT: No concerns were raised during the assessments that required corrective action.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheExternalAgencyInCaseA: Yes, limited assurance has been carried out by external agency (TUV SUD).
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfCoverageAndImplementa: The Company operates treatment plants that enable the reuse of treated water for gardening at most of its facilities. Both Effluent Treatment Plants (ETPs) and Sewage Treatment Plants (STPs) are in place. Out of the 18 plants, 4 are equipped with both ETP and STP, while 15 plants have STP. Additionally, two non-manufacturing plants use a soak pit and septic tank system. The treated water from all these systems is repurposed for gardening. Specifically, Plant 4 in Manesar and Plant 6 in Rudrapur have wastewater treatment systems, with the treated water being sent to the Common Effluent Treatment Plant (CETP).
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheExternalAgencyIfAnyIn: Madhav Associates approved by MoEF. MITCON Consultancy & Engineering Services Ltd. | Pune - MoEF approved lab. Universal analysis lab- NABL – TC5023 – Haryana Win met technologies private limited – UP – MoEF approved lab
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheExternalAgencyThatUnd: Yes, Limited assurance has been carried out by an external agency (TUV SUD)
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfProjectRelatedToReduc:
Yes, Sansera has various projects to reduce greenhouse gas emissions.
Introduced fluke machines to detect leakage in compressors.1200 kW solar rooftop available in 4 plants. SEC (Specific energy consumption) reduction year on year as part of EnMS (Energy management system) journey. Additional 400 KWP solar roof top installed in Plant 5. Wind & Solar energy procurement - 60% of total consumption across all plants (90% in Karnataka plants) Auto cutoff of machinery and equipment power during idle time. Hydraulic Intermittent power pack is Introduced to reduce power consumption. More than 90,000 KWH is saved through washing machine (OMETA Oil) for machines. Usage of Heat pump instead of electric heater for washing machine. Acs is replaced with Inverter Acs. Procurement of IE3 and IE4 motors with 93% to 95% energy efficiency Waste heat recovery by using compressors in washing machines in place of electric heaters Aluminium transient pipes are used for compressed air distribution to reduce pressure loss. Energy efficient air screw compressors procured. Energy efficiency considered in inbuilt SPM machines. Pumps and motors have been optimized for lesser power consumption in Sansera deep hole drilling machines. LPG to PNG conversion in all plants. Photosensors adopted for lightings.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfWasteManagementPracti: Sansera has established the following waste management practices: 1. Segregation of all the waste at source with colour coded bins. 2. Separate storage locations for both Hazardous and non-hazardous waste. (Stored via 3C method- covered, concrete, container) and non-hazardous waste (stored via 3C method) 3. Maintained quantity register for generation and disposal (Form -3, 8 & 10) 4. Sansera has a Monitoring & Measurement Plan where Initiatives like recollection of cotton waste & recollection of used oil by using centrifugal pump, re-use of plastic bins & cans are undertaken. 5. Procedure for waste management Environment Occupational Health and safety Procedure-20 ,23A & 24) is followed.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ReasonsAndCorrectiveActionTake: Sansera does not have any plants located near ecologically sensitive areas, including national parks, wildlife sanctuaries, biosphere reserves, wetlands, biodiversity hotspots, forests and coastal regulation zones.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheExternalAgencyThatUnd: TUV SUD - Scope 3 emissions are considered for three categories such as Employee commuting, Business travel and Waste generated from Operations. Sansera is working on further applicable categories. Over 90% of the waste generated from operations are recycled such as steel scrap, boring scrap, aluminium scrap etc
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfSignificantDirectAndI: SEL does not have any plants around ecologically sensitive areas (such as national parks, wildlife sanctuaries, biosphere reserves, wetlands, biodiversity hotspots, forests and coastal regulation zones, among others). The Company is in industrial areas/estates, as a result its impact on biodiversity is minimal.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DisclosureWebLinkOfEntityAtWhi: The company has established a robust enterprise risk management framework and processes, focusing on the proactive identification and treatment of risks to mitigate their impacts effectively. It allocates adequate resources and management energy towards ensuring business continuity management systems are resilient. Internal systems and capabilities are continually improved with redundancies to enhance operational reliability. The organization adheres to BCMS and Disaster Management Plans aligned with National Disaster Management Authority guidelines, ensuring readiness to manage such circumstances adeptly. Compliance with relevant laws, regulations, and international standards, including ISO 22301, is rigorously maintained across all operational areas. The company prioritizes policy communication, stakeholder training, and regular testing to ensure organizational readiness, with periodic reviews for continuous improvement. Additionally, plans are underway to implement a public addressing system for Plant 12 in 2025.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DiscloseAnySignificantAdverseI: SEL conducts systemic assessments before onboarding a supplier. If the supplier is determined to be at high risk (significant impact to the environment), the supplier is not onboarded. Through this process the Company endeavours to minimise the impact of the value chain on the environment. Wastewater, air quality, noise pollution aspects are covered in these assessments.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple6ExplanatoryText:
1. Total fuel consumption value changed for the year 2023-24 due to re-consideration in PNG conversion factor and mobile consumption data under contract services (Scope 3 emissions). Hence the Energy Intensity changes accordingly.
2. SEL undertakes third-party lab testing for each of these air emission parameters, including NOx and SOx on a quarterly basis at all its locations to ensure the parameters are within permissible limits. This is done in addition to the Company’s internal monitoring systems. The Company also submits the reports to the concerned authority and pollution board
3. Scope 1 for the year 2023-24 changed due to 1) Re-consideration of conversion factor for PNG as it was earlier considered in SCM 2) Previously Lubricant was considered in Scope 1. However, it is considered under scope 3 emission Inventory. 3) Contract services for mobile consumption were considered previously. This changes as the Inventory will fall under scope 3 emissions. 4) As the Scope 1 changes, the emission Intensity for 23-24 also changes accordingly.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InitiativeUndertaken: Efficient preventive maintenance measures for DG and furnaces
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkForDetailsOfInitiativeT: Periodical maintenance is conducted to prevent unexpected breakdowns.
STP - Wastewater is treated and reused for gardening. ETP - Effluents are treated and sent to an authorised agency, with the treated water also used for gardening
Solar and wind power are used for utility power consumption.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InitiativeUndertaken: Eco-friendly coolant used to reduce load on the ETPs
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkForDetailsOfInitiativeT: An eco-friendly coolant is used to extend the life and reduce the operating costs of the ETP.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheArea: Sansera does not withdraw, consume or discharge water in areas of water stress.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NatureOfOperations: Sansera does not withdraw, consume or discharge water in areas of water stress.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheTradeAndIndustryChamb: The Automotive Component Manufacturers Association of India (ACMA)
Bangalore Chamber of Industry and Commerce (BCIC)
Society of Indian Aerospace technologies and industries (SIATI)
Federation of Indian Chambers of Commerce and Industry (FICCI)
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMechanismsToReceive: Investor grievance contact details have been made available on SEL’s website. This is applicable to all stakeholders of the Company including communities. All grievances are resolved within a week. Furthermore, in person complaints made to the respective plants are directed to plant management for further steps and resolution.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:CSRProject: School, Education development programs (23 Government schools)
Scholarship Programs to students from PUC to Degree courses
Education support at Vivek Nagar slum dwelling Girl students (School fees, Evening Snacks)
Blood donation camps through Sansera employees - 618 Units
5S activities Implemented across government schools and Infrastructure development such as painting, Windows, plumbing etc
Co-curricular activities such as Yoga, Music, Physical education etc at government schools
Toilet maintenance programs at Government schools
As part of academics, NMMS special classes conducted for 30 schools
Career Counselling programs conducted for Govt school teachers & students
National Automobile Olympiad 2024 was conducted in 6 govt schools.
Mental health program conducted in 7 Govt schools
Tree Plantation programs at Adugodi Police Station and at Bommasandra Lake
Support to Animal Rehabilitation centre in M/s Mylos at Bengaluru
Scouts & Guides Uniform distribution to 13 Govt schools
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMechanismsInPlaceTo: The Company has a B2B business model. The Company's Quality Team ensures that its customers receive quality products and is also responsive to cater to their grievances if need be. The consumer complaints management system offers several avenues for submission: options include contacting through calls or email IDs. Priority is given to submitting complaints via their portal as the primary method. Alternatively, complaints can be submitted via email as a secondary option.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkOfThePolicyOnCyberSecur: Yes, SEL places a strong emphasis on cyber security. The Company prioritises the protection of official documents and data from unauthorised access. The Company has an Information Technology Acceptable Use Policy and Privacy Policy – these are internal documents for Company use only.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfAnyCorrectiveActionsT: No incidents related to the same have occurred in the assessment year.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:StepsTakenToInformAndEducateCo: Sansera primarily sells its products to Original Equipment Manufacturers (OEMs) and ensures that all products meet the highest quality standards. The company rigorously adheres to regulations concerning banned substances, guaranteeing that none of their products contain materials that are prohibited. This includes full compliance with the Substance of Concern in Products (SoC) guidelines, the Registration, Evaluation, Authorisation, and Restriction of Chemicals (REACH) regulation, and the Restriction of Hazardous Substances (RoHS) directive.
